# Tkinter Cross-Platform Best Practices (Windows + Linux)

Citation-backed research on building tkinter desktop applications that work correctly
on both Windows and Linux. 97 web sources, independently verified.

## TL;DR

| Area | Do This | Why |
|------|---------|-----|
| DPI | `SetProcessDpiAwareness(1)` before `Tk()` on Windows | Default is DPI-unaware = blurry text |
| Theme | sv-ttk + darkdetect | Consistent dark/light across OSes |
| Fonts | Negative (pixel) sizes + fallback chain | Point sizes render differently per platform |
| Borderless | Expect focus/stacking issues on Linux | `overrideredirect` bypasses the window manager |
| Modal | `transient()` → `wait_visibility()` → `grab_set()` | `grab_set()` alone is unreliable on Linux |
| Tray | pystray + check `Icon.HAS_MENU` at runtime | GNOME has no tray without a user-installed extension |
| Scrolling | Bind `<MouseWheel>` AND `<Button-4/5>` | Linux uses different events than Windows |
| Packaging | PyInstaller `--onefile --noconsole` | Broadest cross-platform support |
| Wayland | Know what breaks (positioning, tray) | No native Tk support; XWayland fallback only |

## Quick Decision Framework

1. Add Windows DPI call at app startup — one line, fixes the #1 visual bug
2. Pick a theme library (sv-ttk recommended) — eliminates per-platform styling work
3. Define font fallback chains per platform — Segoe UI (Win) / Noto Sans (Linux)
4. Test modal dialogs on Linux — `grab_set()` is unreliable across window managers
5. Never rely solely on the system tray — provide keyboard/menu alternatives
6. Bind both scroll event types — or wait for Tk 8.7 (TIP 474)
7. Use PyInstaller for packaging; Nuitka if startup time matters
